content: Apple Inc. was founded on April 1, 1976, by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne in Jobs' parents' garage. This marked the beginning of the company, which later introduced the Apple I computer in June 1976.
raw_content: Apple Inc. was founded on April 1, 1976, by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne in Jobs' parents' garage. This marked the beginning of the company, which later introduced the Apple I computer in June 1976.
reasoning:
Okay, so I need to figure out when Apple Inc. was founded. I remember hearing that Apple is a pretty old company in the tech world, but I'm not exactly sure of the exact year. Let me think about what I know.

I know that Apple is associated with Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak. They were the co-founders, right? I think they started the company in the 1970s because that's when a lot of the personal computer revolution began. Maybe around 1976 or 1977? I'm trying to recall if there was a specific event or product that marks the founding.

Wait, wasn't there something about them starting in a garage? Yeah, that's a common story. So, if it was in a garage, that might have been in the mid-70s. I also remember that Apple introduced the Apple I computer, which was one of their first products. I think that came out in 1976. So maybe the company was founded the same year as the Apple I.

I'm pretty sure it's 1976, but I should double-check. Sometimes companies have incorporation dates that are a bit after they started operating, but in Apple's case, I believe the founding date is when they officially started, which would be April 1, 1976. That makes sense because the Apple I was released later that year, in June or July.

So putting it all together, I think Apple Inc. was founded in 1976 by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne. They started in Jobs' parents' garage and introduced their first computer the same year. I don't think it's any other year because the 70s were the starting point for many tech companies, and Apple is one of the pioneers.
